questions.text: What is the source of the dataset? (e.g. Proprietary vs open source, primary vs secondary source)
What are the legal ramifications of the dataset source?
What is the data repository? (public repository where the dataset can be found)
Who owns the dataset?
Which institutes/organisations are the dataset owners affiliated with? If so, are there any data (sharing) agreements that are or should be consolidated?
Who has access to the data/ the datasets? (internally & externally)
Was the dataset split? If so, how?
Which dataset did you use for validation?
What is the purpose this data will be used for (use cases)?
Is the volume of data collected proportional to the scope of the intended purpose?
What are suitable use cases for this dataset?
What are unsuitable use-cases for this dataset?
What form is this data in? (e.g. structured, unstructured, semi-structured, etc)
What are the data types present in the dataset? (e.g. image, audio, text, video, etc.)
Does the dataset contain any special categories of personal data under GDPR? If yes, what kind?
Is the dataset free of errors?
Who has taken responsibility to evaluate and check the dataset? What methodologies were used?
Are there any inconsistencies in the dataset?
Are there any duplicate entries?
Are there any missing values?
Are there any significant outliers or anomalies?
Is there version consistency?
Are the units of measurement consistent throughout the dataset?
Are the data formats consistent throughout the dataset?
What demographic information and domain is the dataset aiming to represent?
What is the minimum sample size to represent the population sufficiently and significantly?
Is the dataset sufficiently large to capture the variability and complexity of the underlying phenomena? Is the dataset representative of the target population or domain?
Are there enough samples from minority or underrepresented groups to avoid biases and ensure fairness?
Does the dataset reflect temporal changes, and are those changes relevant?
Is the dataset being updated to reflect evolving trends and changes?
Has the dataset been checked for harmful biases?
Is the dataset sufficiently representative in view of the intended purpose?
Does the dataset cover the full scope of scenarios the AI system will encounter?
If the dataset contained personal data, how was the data features anonymised or pseudonymised?
How was this data collected? What collection methods were used?
What were the sampling methods used?
Could any of the sampling methods biased towards a particular group? If yes, is this bias necessary to fulfil the intended purpose?
What are specific and appropriate measures that address the identified bias?
When was this data collected?
What were the general criteria for including the selected data features/data points?
What data features/data points were excluded? Based on what criteria?
Has the dataset been checked for the individual data features? Are those correct and complete?
Volume: What is the volume of the data?
What volume of data is necessary for the development of the AI system, keeping in mind the data minimisation principle?
Velocity: What is the speed at which the data arrives?
Variety: How varied are the data types and sources?
What is the data sample size?
Were (parts of) the dataset(s) unavailable?
What data processing techniques were used? (e.g. cleaning, normalisation, standardization)
Was data labelling used? Was the labelling manual or automatic? What are the ground truths attached to each label?
Are the data processing techniques appropriate, given the intended purposes? If no, which data processing elements are inappropriate and why?
What data processing tools were used?
Are there any inconsistencies in the data processing that might affect the completeness of the dataset?
Have any possible biases been identified at the level of data preparation processing operations that may impact the datasets?
What are specific and appropriate measures that address the identified bias?
What are the measures of central tendency? Do these measures accurately represent the dataset?
What is the variability of the dataset around the central tendency? Are there any outliers or extreme values present?
Are there significant differences in statistical properties within the dataset?

What are the assumptions that are formulated with respect to the information the dataset is supposed to represent and measure? Are these assumptions appropriate, reasonable and fair in light of the AI system's intended purpose?
What is the rationale behind these assumptions if you look at the people that the system is going to be used for? (ANNEX IV 2(b))
Do the relevant formulated assumptions target a specific vulnerable group? And are those formulations likely to harm that group? If so, how can the formulations be adjusted?
Will the dataset provide meaningful insights or outcomes? If so, what are they?
Are there any outlier cases that need to be included in the dataset?
Have any possible biases that are likely to affect the health of persons been identified as a result of this examination?
Have any possible biases that are likely to affect the safety of persons been identified as a result of this examination?
What is the scale of the data feature?
Can the scale introduce errors/bias/unfairness/unsafety into the system? If so, how?
What is the volume of the data feature?
Can the volume introduce errors/bias/unfairness/unsafety into the system? If so, how?
What is the distribution of the data feature? Is the distribution representative in view of the intended purpose?
Is the information provided by the data feature necessary and proportionate for the intended purpose?
What is the information in the data feature supposed to measure and represent?
What are the assumptions that are formulated with respect to the information the data feature is supposed to represent and measure?
Where do these assumptions come from? Are there any historical, social, or cultural biases that underpin these assumptions?
Are there any missing elements re. the data feature?
Does the data feature, considering its scale, provide a complete view/ representation of the group(s) it aims to represent?
Does the data feature, considering its volume provide a complete view/representation of the group(s) it aims to represent?
Is the feature a characteristic that is relevant for the geographical setting of the system
In light of the system's intended purpose, are there geographical elements that should be taken into account re. the data feature?
In light of the system's intended purpose, are there contextual elements that should be taken into account re. the data feature?
In light of the system's intended purpose, are there behavioral elements that should be taken into account re. the data feature?
In light of the system's intended purpose, are there functional elements that should be taken into account re. the data feature?
Are there any interactions with other features that could collectively introduce or amplify any biases or unfair outcomes that may not be apparent when considering a data feature in isolation?
Within the context of the data point, is the information provided by the data feature necessary and proportionate for the intended purposes? Could interactions with other features negate this?
Can the combination of multiple sensitive data features increase the discrimination potential?
Is the data variable/feature considered personal data under the GDPR?
Is the data variable/feature considered a special category of personal data under the GDPR?
Is the processing of the special category personal data variable/feature strictly necessary for bias detection / correction? Could other data (including synthetic / anonymised) fulfil it?
Do the processing activity records adequately document and reflect the reasons for processing this data feature, including why the alternatives are insufficient?
Are there technical limitations on reuse, and privacy‑preserving measures (pseudonymisation etc.) in place? For example, has personal data been non-reversibly anonymized?
Are access controls, documentation of access, and confidentiality obligations in place for this special category personal data feature?
Is there a deletion or retention plan for this special category personal data feature, to ensure it is removed once bias has been corrected or the retention period has expired?
Are there safeguards to ensure the data will not be transmitted or otherwise accessed by unauthorised parties?
Is the data variable/feature prohibited ground for discrimination (Art. 21 European Charter of Fundamental Rights)?
Could the data variable/feature infringe upon other equality, non-discrimination, fair treatment rights?

Could the data variable/feature be a proxy for a prohibited ground of discrimination, or lead to infringement of other equality, non-discrimination, and fair treatment rights? (Art. 21 European Charter of Fundamental Rights)
